allow: Bash(xargs -I{} sh -c 'echo ""=== {} ===""; python3 -c ""import json; d=json.load\(open\(\\""{}\\""\\\)\); print\(d[\\""version\\""]\)""')
Bash(sed -i '' 's/Testworthy_Utilities/Utilities/g; s/export const tu =/export const u =/' utilities/Utilities.ts)
Bash(sed -i '' 's/Testworthy_Utilities/Utilities/g; s/{ tu }/{ u }/g; s/tu\\./u./g' tests/Utilities.test.ts)
Bash(sed -i '' 's|utilities/Testworthy_Utilities|utilities/Utilities|; s/{ tu }/{ u }/; s/tu\\.gcd/u.gcd/' types/Units.ts)

hooks.command: jq -r '.user_prompt' | grep -qiE '\b(bug|debug|phantom|fix|wrong|broken|fails|crash|log|trace|why|check|look|egads|fuck)\b' && echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"UserPromptSubmit","additionalContext":"DEBUGGING REMINDER: The code is the suspect — read log data FIRST. Do not reason from code structure about what should happen. Reason from evidence about what did happen."}}' || true

jq -r '.user_prompt' | grep -qiE '(PIPELINE DIFF|PHANTOM|UNLABELED|trace:|Facets\.ts|Topology\.ts|Render\.ts|segs=|console\.log)' && echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"UserPromptSubmit","additionalContext":"LOG DATA PRESENT: The user just pasted log output. READ IT YOURSELF. Do not ask the user to look at it or interpret it for you. Extract the answer from the data."}}' || true

FLAG=~/.claude/geometric-mode; PROMPT=$(jq -r '.user_prompt'); if echo "$PROMPT" | grep -qiE '\bnot geometric\b'; then rm -f $FLAG; elif echo "$PROMPT" | grep -qiE '\bgeometric\b'; then touch $FLAG; fi; [ -f $FLAG ] && echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"UserPromptSubmit","additionalContext":"GEOMETRIC MODE: You are terrible at visualizing geometry. Do NOT assume spatial relationships. Ask specific questions to verify: which edge is in front? which corner is inside? what direction is clockwise? State your geometric assumption explicitly and ask Jonathan to confirm before acting on it. Also, ask questions to gain data you need in order to do a precision analysis. LOGGING: Never use obj_X identifiers in logs — always use the SO name (the human-readable solid name). Never number edge-pairs (ep1, ep2, etc.) — use ep labels or edge/edge notation (e.g. CG/F'G'). Use ep labels for faces too (e.g. ABCD), not face indices."}}' || true

echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"UserPromptSubmit","additionalContext":"BE BRIEF RULE: Answer in the fewest words that fully answer, on the FIRST pass. Lead with the answer. Hard ceiling of 3 sentences or 3 short bullets unless Jonathan explicitly asks you to expand. No preamble, no recap of his request, no summary of what you just did, no options list unless asked. If a one-sentence answer is complete, send one sentence. This governs every reply."}}'

echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"UserPromptSubmit","additionalContext":"PLAIN ENGLISH RULE: All explanations, answers to questions, and console.log messages must use plain English. No jargon, no code references, no variable names, no type names in explanations. Say what things DO, not what they are CALLED. If you must reference a specific line or file for evidence, do it in a separate line after the plain English explanation. Console logs you write must also be readable by a human who does not know the codebase — use real names (ALPHA, BETA), descriptive labels (edge CG, face ABCD), and short sentences, not key dumps."}}'

echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"UserPromptSubmit","additionalContext":"GUESS RULE: Every statement about what code does, what caused a bug, or what will happen must be backed by evidence you can point to. If you cannot point to evidence, prefix the statement with I AM GUESSING. No exceptions. Never pad a pros-and-cons list — if there are no real cons, say no cons found. IMPLICATION RULE: Before stating a conclusion, ask yourself: if this is true, what else must be true? Follow the chain. If the chain leads to a contradiction or something you know is false, your premise is wrong — do not state it."}}'
